title: [Claude Code] MCP Filesystem: Read and Write Files via Agent
description: Set up the MCP filesystem server in Claude Code and build an agent that reads project files, analyzes them, and writes output files autonomously.

steps.title: Add the MCP filesystem server
steps.instruction: Configure the MCP filesystem server so Claude Code can read and write files in your project directory.
steps.prompt: How do I add the MCP filesystem server to Claude Code? Give me the exact command.
steps.expected_result: MCP filesystem server running and connected to Claude Code
steps.tip: Run `claude mcp list` to verify the server is active

steps.title: Read and analyze files
steps.instruction: Ask Claude Code to read all files in a directory and produce a summary.
steps.prompt: Read all TypeScript files in the /src directory. For each file, tell me: what it does, its dependencies, and any potential improvements.
steps.expected_result: A structured analysis of all TypeScript files in the directory
steps.tip: Try this on a real project directory — the more context, the better the analysis

steps.title: Write an output file
steps.instruction: Have Claude Code write its analysis to a new markdown file.
steps.prompt: Write the analysis from the previous step to a file called PROJECT_ANALYSIS.md. Use clear headings for each file.
steps.expected_result: A PROJECT_ANALYSIS.md file created in your project directory
steps.tip: Check the file after creation with `cat PROJECT_ANALYSIS.md`

challenge: Build a Claude Code automation that monitors a /drafts folder, reads each markdown draft, improves the writing, and saves the improved version to /published — all in a single session.

videos.title: Claude Code MCP Filesystem Tutorial
videos.search_keyword: Claude Code MCP filesystem server tutorial 2026

practicePrompts.label: Set Up MCP Server
practicePrompts.instruction: Initialize the MCP filesystem server in Claude Code.
practicePrompts.prompt: Set up the MCP filesystem server in Claude Code and ensure it is running correctly.
practicePrompts.followUp: Try adding different configurations to the server setup.

practicePrompts.label: Read Project Files
practicePrompts.instruction: Create an agent to read a project file.
practicePrompts.prompt: Build an agent that reads the contents of a specified project file in the MCP filesystem.
practicePrompts.followUp: Modify the agent to read multiple files and summarize their contents.

practicePrompts.label: Write Output Files
practicePrompts.instruction: Develop an agent that writes results to a file.
practicePrompts.prompt: Create an agent that analyzes project file data and writes the results to a new output file.
practicePrompts.followUp: Experiment with different output formats for the results.

evaluation.prompt: Paste your lab output, generated code, or error log here and AI will evaluate whether the lab is progressing correctly.
evaluation.context: This lab is "[Claude Code] MCP Filesystem: Read and Write Files via Agent". Set up the MCP filesystem server in Claude Code and build an agent that reads project files, analyzes them, and writes output files autonomously.
successCriteria: The learner completed the main lab step
The output is concrete enough to inspect
The result shows progress toward a working implementation
commonMistakes: Not including the real output or error message
Stopping after setup without testing the actual task
